_description: Rolling history of Claude Schefter's recent posts. Used to prevent repetition and enable callbacks. Scanner appends to this file after each post; oldest entries get pruned to keep the file capped at 30.

entry.subject: short tag — which owner/team/topic was the focus
entry.openerUsed: the opener phrase used (for rotation avoidance)
entry.reframeMode: telemetry — which hostile-tip frame the post used: '' | style-book | intra-division | reverse-lens | rivalry | league-office

posts.body: A familiar voice on the line is picking up heat on Vitside's recent moves—somebody in the Southwest thinks the Mafia's playing with fire. We'll see how that one settles.
posts.subject: web

posts.body: Multiple Northwest owners sending complaints toward the league office this week. Developing.
posts.subject: franchise (Vitside)

posts.body: Sources tell me something's brewing — too early to say what, but the phones are warm. Developing.
posts.subject: web

posts.body: Multiple Northwest owners sending complaints toward the league office this week. Developing.
posts.subject: franchise (Vitside)

posts.body: The Northwest is the most active corner of the league this week. Several desks pushing the same story. More to come.
posts.subject: franchise (Vitside)

posts.body: Up and at 'em. Hearing specific heat from the Central desk—Cowboy Up's Josh Jacobs deal (4 years) is already singing the Blues. First naming this month for the Cowboy. Floor's open, Cowboy Up.
posts.subject: web

posts.body: Per a familiar voice: Vitside's workshopping some unconventional roster alchemy while the league office gears up for a counter move. The madness, apparently, is contagious. More to come.
posts.subject: web

posts.body: League sources tell me the Northwest is buzzing — multiple owners whispering the same tune. Developing.
posts.subject: franchise (Vitside)

posts.body: Hearing the league office is catching static this week. Not all of it about roster moves. Usually tells you more about the accuser than the accused. Developing.
posts.subject: web

posts.body: Vitside's officially on the block for trade offers—multiple sources confirm the Mafia's shopping. League office already had to remind owners that 'send me your best offers' is a trade solicitation, not an invitation to creative negotiation. Floor's open.
posts.subject: franchise (Vitside)

posts.body: Morning, league. Hearing a team in the Northwest is catching some heat from a corner of the Southwest—not all of it about fantasy football. Usually tells you more about the accuser than the accused.
posts.subject: division (Northwest)

posts.body: Vitside shocked the league going Omar Cooper Jr. over tight end—the room's still processing the pivot. After weeks of seam-route obsession, the Mafia just said 'forget tight, I need wide.' Floor's open, Vitside.
posts.subject: web

posts.body: Pour yourself a cup. Word from the East: this draft class is thin enough that future-pick trades might be off the menu entirely. If that holds, it changes the calculus on deadline moves. We'll see.
posts.subject: web

posts.body: Multiple sources say Vitside's zeroing in on a tight end that can win down the seam—the kind of vertical threat that pairs with the Mafia's receiver-heavy build. At this point, it's not if they move on TE, it's when.
posts.subject: franchise (Vitside)

posts.body: Vitside's tight end strategy is drawing heat from across the league—multiple sources confirm a Northwest desk has its sights set on the Mafia's moves at the position, and the cracks are showing. Pick 1.12 is the pressure point; somebody's about to call.
posts.subject: franchise (Vitside)

posts.body: Hearing the Pigskins are exploring a trade-down scenario. Brandon's apparently working the phones on draft capital. Developing.
posts.subject: web

posts.body: Morning, league. Vitside's tight end obsession is getting creative — sources describe the prep regimen as 'high reps, short rests, explosive finishes,' all while the Mafia insists there's nothing weird about it. Pick 1.12 looms large; the question isn't TE or WR anymore, it's how committed they really are. Developing.
posts.subject: franchise (Vitside)

posts.body: Per league sources: the Pigskins' tight end room has caught some attention from the commissioner's office. Brandon's apparently got thoughts on whether the depth holds. More to come.
posts.subject: web

posts.body: Vitside's wrestling with a familiar problem: tight end or emerging wideout. The Mafia's leaning into the TE uncertainty, talking about playing things 'fast and loose' at the position. That's usually code for 'open to offers.' We'll see.
posts.subject: franchise (Vitside)

posts.body: Multiple sources tell me Vitside's zeroed in on tight end and won't stop shopping until they land a difference-maker—even if it costs them a premium pick. The Mafia's got specifics in mind. We'll see if the price tag sticks.
posts.subject: franchise (Vitside)

posts.body: Vitside's flipped on tight end — suddenly convinced they've got the strongest at the position. Confidence is one thing. We'll see if it holds. One to watch.
posts.subject: web

posts.body: I'm told trade offers are moving across the board—Vitside shopping from a position of need at depth, Midwest open for business on picks. The marketplace is live. One to watch.
posts.subject: web

posts.body: Hearing the Southwest division is buzzing about something. Developing.
posts.subject: division (Southwest)

posts.body: Vit's got a specific need at tight end and isn't shy about shopping for it. Hearing the Mafia's willing to move pieces to fill that hole. Stay tuned.
posts.subject: web

posts.body: Per source: the Geeks are zeroed in on RBs this draft, and the board figures to move early and often. Multiple teams working angles to shift picks. Nothing imminent, but the phones are already warm. More to come.
posts.subject: web

posts.body: Hearing owners are queuing up Draft Day for the annual preseason ritual. Sonny Weaver no matter what — that's the card being prepped across the league. Meanwhile, somebody in the Central division is spending so much time in the basement that pneumonia's a real concern. Classic August behavior. Developing.
posts.subject: division (Central)
